credits.name: Georgia Retraining Tax Credit
credits.rationale: Sector software stack indicates periodic upskilling for systems adoption and process changes.
assumptions: Company has Georgia payroll tax liability

credits.name: Federal R&D Tax Credit
credits.rationale: Website/notes include engineering, integration, or process-improvement signals.

credits.name: Georgia R&D Tax Credit
credits.rationale: Georgia credit follows similar qualified research principles with state-specific base calculations.
assumptions: Prior year spend available for base-period computation

credits.name: Cost Segregation Study
credits.rationale: Property acquisition/improvement signals suggest opportunity to accelerate depreciation.
triggers: No explicit purchase event found; recommend confirmation
assumptions: Commercial property acquired, built, or improved

credits.name: Georgia Investment Tax Credit
credits.rationale: Expansion/capital signals combined with county tier may support Georgia investment incentives.
triggers: Need verification of recent capital investment in GA
assumptions: Qualified investment property placed in service

credits.name: Self-Insured Medical Reimbursement Plan (MERP)
credits.rationale: Most mid-size employers can evaluate MERP/HRA-style structures for payroll tax and benefit efficiency.

narrative.ga_jtc_intro: The GA JTC offers a dollar-for-dollar reduction of state income tax liability when creating new jobs in certain areas. Counties are designated Tier 1 through Tier 4, with Military Zones, Less Developed Census Tracts (LDCT), and Opportunity Zones often supporting lower thresholds and larger benefits.
narrative.ga_jtc_note: T Management Georgia locations with corresponding tier designations and estimated credit benefits are shown below. Final thresholds may be NAICS dependent and should be validated.
narrative.retraining_intro: The GA Retraining Tax Credit can provide up to 50% of eligible retraining costs, including wages of trainees, up to $1,250 per employee per year.
narrative.retraining_context: There are many software systems and equipment platforms in the healthcare space that could qualify. If T Management implemented new systems or equipment and retrained existing employees, there may be meaningful savings available.
narrative.rd_intro: Federal and Georgia R&D credits can provide tax relief on qualified technical activities, typically as a percentage of qualified research spend including eligible wages.
narrative.rd_examples_intro: Examples of how T Management may qualify include:
narrative.costseg_intro: T Management may be able to reduce taxes through cost segregation by accelerating depreciation on newly constructed, expanded, or purchased facilities.
narrative.costseg_detail: For operations-heavy facilities, portions such as site improvements, electrical systems, specialized power, fabrication areas, and certain interior improvements may qualify for shorter depreciation lives.
narrative.costseg_bonus: A substantial portion of commercial building basis (net of land) may be accelerated in year one, subject to current bonus depreciation rules and project facts.
